Like the big sheet. One of the big secrets that Laura taught me was really early on it was like just to finish
a draft and not like go back and edit everything. Like if I, cause once you've completed like a draft,
whether it's an email or a sales letter, it's just a lot easier to go in and tighten up things and tweak lines
and things like that. But if you're constantly stopping and revising lines and editing and doing that, you
get in your own head and you end up just sort of, uh, it becomes a mess. And if you finish a draft, you
always feel like, Oh, it's actually not as bad as I thought it was while I was writing it. And then you can
make it a lot stronger. So, so what you to that point, like it is a joke on this call, but if for, for people who
